question: How does Flask dispatch a request to a view function?

graph.answer:
Flask dispatches a request in a short pipeline:

- `wsgi_app()` creates the request context, pushes it, and then calls `full_dispatch_request()`; see [src/flask/app.py](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/flask/src/flask/app.py#L1566).
- Pushing the request context runs `match_request()`, which calls `url_adapter.match(return_rule=True)` and stores the result on the request as `request.url_rule` and `request.view_args`; see [src/flask/ctx.py](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/flask/src/flask/ctx.py#L405) and [src/flask/wrappers.py](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/flask/src/flask/wrappers.py#L43).
- `full_dispatch_request()` emits `request_started`, runs `preprocess_request()`, and only calls the view if no `before_request` handler returned a value; see [src/flask/app.py](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/flask/src/flask/app.py#L992) and [src/flask/app.py](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/flask/src/flask/app.py#L1366).
- `dispatch_request()` then uses the matched rule and endpoint to look up the callable in `self.view_functions`, and invokes it with `**request.view_args` through `ensure_sync()` so async views work too; see [src/flask/app.py](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/flask/src/flask/app.py#L966) and [src/flask/sansio/scaffold.py](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/flask/src/flask/sansio/scaffold.py#L108).
- The return value is then normalized by `finalize_request()`, which runs response processing and teardown; see [src/flask/app.py](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/flask/src/flask/app.py#L1021).

One practical detail: Flask does not dispatch by path directly at call time. It dispatches by endpoint name, using the route match that was already stored on the request.

For class-based views:
- `View.as_view()` creates the callable that gets stored in `view_functions`, and `MethodView.dispatch_request()` chooses the HTTP-method-specific handler; see [src/flask/views.py](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/flask/src/flask/views.py#L16) and [src/flask/views.py](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/flask/src/flask/views.py#L182).

If you want, I can also trace the exact path for a normal function view vs a `MethodView` in the source.
